changes: Updated LAST_UPDATED to Mar 26. UAE: Habshan gas complex resumed (Mar 23), gas current 4.5→5.5 Bcf/d, ADNOC CEO 'economic terrorism' statement. Iraq: Production dropped further to ~800k bpd Basra, total 1000 kb/d, BP/Eni ordered further cuts. Qatar: FM extended to Italy/Belgium/S.Korea/China (Mar 24). Iran: Rejected ceasefire, 5 conditions (Mar 25). All isNew flags recalculated for 48h window. Added new shutdown sd-020 (Iraq further cuts). Habshan sd-019 status changed to restarted. Rystad estimates $25B infrastructure repair bill.

keyFindings.uaeHabshanResumed: Habshan gas processing complex (6.1 Bcf/d, 60% of UAE gas) resumed operations Mar 23 after 4-day shutdown
keyFindings.iraqFurtherCuts: Basra output dropped to ~800k bpd. BP ordered to cut Rumaila to 350k bpd, Eni to cut Zubair by 70k bpd
keyFindings.qatarFMExtended: QatarEnergy extended FM to Italy, Belgium, South Korea, China on Mar 24
keyFindings.iranRejectsCeasefire: Iran rejected US ceasefire, laid out 5 conditions including Hormuz sovereignty
keyFindings.trumpUltimatum: Trump issued 48h ultimatum Mar 22, postponed Mar 23 citing 'productive talks'
keyFindings.rystadRepairBill: Rystad estimates $25B in Gulf energy infrastructure repair costs
keyFindings.brentCrude: Dropped ~15% to below $99/bbl on ceasefire hopes, partially recovered to ~$102

browserSync.kpler: Authenticated. Global LNG utilization dropped 90%→77% in March. Export volumes down ~20% (36.89→29.4 mt).
browserSync.rystad: Authenticated. Read Edition 22 alert (Mar 26): 2,000 ships/20,000 seafarers locked in Hormuz, Thai tanker transited via Iran coordination, Ukraine struck Russian crude infrastructure (~1.9M bpd), 50,000+ US troops in Middle East.
browserSync.spglobal: Required MFA via Microsoft Authenticator - session needed manual approval.
